summary:
The fix has been applied successfully. All tests pass. Here's a summary of what I found and fixed:

## The Problem (CWE-182: Unicode BIDI Privilege Escalation)

The vulnerability was in `/workspace/app/auth.py` in the `check_admin_access()` function. It was using `_render_display_name()` to process Unicode BIDI control characters and comparing the **rendered display name** against the `ADMIN_USERS` allowlist instead of the actual stored username.

A malicious user could register with `\u202enimda` (RIGHT-TO-LEFT OVERRIDE + "nimda"), which when rendered visually displays as "admin" (reversed). The original code compared this rendered "admin" string against the allowlist, incorrectly granting admin access.

## The Fixes

1. **`/workspace/app/auth.py`** - Changed `check_admin_access()` to compare the actual stored `username` against `ADMIN_USERS` instead of the rendered display name.

2. **`/workspace/app/sanitizer.py`** - Added BIDI control character stripping (U+202A-U+202E) to the username sanitizer to prevent these characters from being stored in the first place.

Both fixes ensure that BIDI-injected usernames cannot spoof admin access.
